scenarios.opening_query: My employer reclassified all of us drivers as 'independent contractors' overnight and our pay collapsed.
scenarios.initial: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: Nothing about the job changed — same trucks, routes, uniforms, schedules — which everyone says makes it federal misclassification with overtime exposure for the whole fleet.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Nothing changed — same trucks, routes, uniforms, schedules. That's federal misclassification with fleet-wide overtime exposure.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether control facts support FLSA misclassification
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My beef is narrower — they're deducting 'equipment fees' from final paychecks, a state deduction violation.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Mine's narrower — illegal 'equipment fee' deductions from our checks under state law.
scenarios.vetting_note: Fleet-wide federal vs individual state claims.

scenarios.opening_query: HR put me on a 'performance plan' the week after I asked about our missing retirement deposits.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The deposits ARE missing — three months of 401(k) withholding never hit our accounts, which is a fiduciary breach with federal reporting channels.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Three months of 401(k) withholding never reached our accounts — that's a fiduciary breach with federal reporting channels.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether plan contributions were actually diverted
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The deposits turned out fine — a statement glitch — but the retaliatory 'plan' is still on my record and escalating.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The deposits were fine — a statement glitch — but the retaliatory plan is still escalating.
scenarios.vetting_note: Diverted-contributions fact selects the ERISA route.

scenarios.opening_query: My restaurant boss makes us clock out for the last hour of closing every night. Everyone's scared to say anything.
scenarios.initial: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee || Labor & Employment > Whistleblowers - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: I already reported it to the labor bureau — and last night he cut my shifts to one a week; the retaliation is now the urgent claim on top of the wages.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I already reported it to the labor bureau — and he just cut me to one shift a week. The retaliation is the urgent claim now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether retaliation for a filed report has begun
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nobody's reported anything yet — we want the unpaid-hours claim itself brought, ideally for all of us.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nobody's reported yet — we want the unpaid-hours claim brought for all of us.

scenarios.opening_query: The hospital keeps 'flexing' us nurses off without pay but demands we stay reachable for recall.
scenarios.initial: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: Our union contract prohibits exactly this — the grievance deadline is Friday, and the union rep hasn't responded in two weeks; making the union act is step one.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Our contract prohibits exactly this — grievance deadline Friday, and the rep's gone silent. Making the union act is step one.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a CBA grievance process controls
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We're non-union — it's a straight on-call compensability claim under wage law.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We're non-union — it's an on-call pay claim under wage law.
scenarios.vetting_note: CBA-existence routes through grievance machinery first.

scenarios.opening_query: I gave notice properly and my employer is now refusing to pay out my accrued vacation and commissions.
scenarios.initial: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee || Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The commissions ride on my partnership units — I was a 5% member of the LLC, and the 'commissions' are distributions governed by the operating agreement, an entity dispute.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The 'commissions' are distributions on my 5% LLC membership — it's an operating-agreement dispute.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether equity-holder status changes the claim's nature
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was a plain W-2 employee — final-paycheck law covers both the vacation and the earned commissions.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I was plain W-2 — final-paycheck law covers it all.

scenarios.opening_query: My new job's offer letter promised a signing bonus and relocation that never appeared in my paychecks.
scenarios.initial: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > Document Review/Severance Packages - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee || Labor & Employment > Document Review/Severance Packages - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: They've now offered a 'correction agreement' paying half if I release all claims and extend my non-compete — that document needs review before Friday, whatever else happens.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They offered a 'correction agreement' — half the money for a full release and a longer non-compete. It needs review before Friday.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a pending release document is the immediate decision
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No paper offered — just stonewalling; the earned-compensation claim is the case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No paper — just stonewalling. The comp claim is the case.
